title: Provide option to bypass auto proxy environment handling.  Especially for machine itself.
body:
### Feature request description

When I 'podman start machine' it always updates my machine with however my environment is configured in my current shell I issue that command from.  If I don't have a proxy settings, it somehow reverts to some old values I was using in podman desktop, even though I uninstalled it.  But that's secondary.

In my environment that doesn't work and I need to set the proxies in the containers and leave them alone.  I would like to have that at least be an optional argument.  Either to skip proxy envs, or skip any automatic envs it may be handling now maybe?  Either way would be very helpful.

### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

podman machine start --pass_env=false
podman machine start --no_proxy_env

Anything of that nature.  Then whatever was in /etc./profile.d/default-env.sh (or any other location I have modified) will remain referencing the same proxy I initially configured it for.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

Doing it manually.
Scripting on the windows host:
podman machine down && wsl --shutdown && source my_proxy_setup.sh && podman machine start

Scripting on boot of the WSL machine (this might get overwritten - idk timing):
cp /etc/profile.d/MY.default-env.sh /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh

I haven't thought of anything more elegant and haven't looked into the code at all.

### Additional context

I'm sure it's a useful feature for many but it's proving pretty difficult to work around in a situation where it isn't wanted.

comments.body:
Actually it looks like podman may be looking at podman-desktop's config files?  Would that be a bug?  Somehow when I "podman start instance" it's resurrecting the properties that are in C:\Users\<me>\.local\share\containers\podman-desktop\configuration

I wouldn't expect those to be referenced like that even if I did still have desktop installed.  WIth it uninstalled - it feels especially undesirable.

This would be very weird, since Podman knows nothing of podman desktop.  Look for containers.conf file though, that would be where customizations are happening.
$HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf and $HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf.d/*

If you remove the podman-desktop\configuration does the issue go away?  Another thought would be this configuraiton is stored within the podman machine/WSL instance.

Looks like you're right, I removed that podman-desktop config and it's still doing it.  So I can't figure out where it's coming from.  It's a made up dummy proxy URL that I only ever entered into the podman-desktop settings when I was trying it.  So have no idea how it could be springing back up into my WSL machine.... that was the only place I cold find it still referenced.

And in windows I don't seem to have any other config files in $HOME/.config or $HOME/.local besides that one for podman-desktop (and the machine .json file in $HOME\.config\containers\podman\machine\wsl that doesn't talk about proxies).

I'm looking at /etc/profile.d/default-env.sh in the WSL podman default machine.

If I set proxy vars in my git-bash shell, then run podman machine stop/start, that file in the WSL machine will take on whatever proxy vars I have in my git bash shell.

If I clear/unset all proxy env vars (not set to blank - unset), somehow that file reverts to that value I used once in podman desktop as a test.  A totally made up value I don't use anywhere else.  So I can't figure out where it's coming from.  Or more importantly, I can't figure out how to make it stop coming back or changing every time I stop/start the machine again.

Can I make it just leave that default-env.sh file alone.... let me manage it manually like a traditional VM without any non-standard behind the scenes automation attempts?

I noticed that bringing up and down the machine with wsl commands directly it does not change the default-env.sh.  It appears to make the change at podman machine start.  Only way I found to have always set to what I need is to wrap it in a script that shuts podman and wsl down, sets proxy vars, then podman machine start.  That updates the default-env.sh in the WSL and I'm good until reboot as long as I don't start and stop it all the time.

So there's no way to do this currently?  Any idea if it's intentional to not allow it to be turned off, or might a PR be considered if I saw a simple way to add it the option?

fwiw, this is windows again, and our proxy is at localhost in windows because my corp overlords use a proxy tunnel/client app.  From within the WSL (with user-mode-networking due to VPN) localhost doesn't point back to the host, so it has to use a different proxy.  The podman machine auto proxy behavior keeps changing it back on me if I don't start it with a script that explicitly sets the proxy vars before starting the machine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

AFAIK podman-desktop writes the proxies to containers.conf

So you should make sure to delete it from there, on windoes the path should be under `APPDATA\containers\containers.conf` or possible in a `.d` subdirectory.

But yes you are right on each start the machine is updated with the current proxy env, but I think you could very well already unset the env var on each start to avoid that. It may also be possible to set the vars to empty values in containers.conf to configure this permanently (although I haven't tried that)
